info.name: Brello HRMS API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Brello HRMS multi-tenant, multi-app backend.

## Setup
1. Set the `base_url` variable to your server address (default: `http://localhost:8000`)
2. Start with the **Login** request to get tokens
3. Tokens are auto-saved to collection variables for authenticated requests

## Authentication Flow
1. Login → saves `access_token` and `refresh_token`
2. Use authenticated endpoints (token is auto-injected)
3. Switch App → saves new `access_token`
4. Refresh → rotates both tokens

exec: console.log('✅ Tokens saved. Default app:', data.defaultAppId);

request.description:
Authenticate with email and password.

**Auto-saves:** `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `user_id`, `enterprise_id`, `organization_id`, `default_app_id` to collection variables.

Switch to a different application. Provide the target `appId` (get available apps from the Login response).

**Auto-saves:** New `access_token` scoped to the target app.

Refresh the access token using the refresh token.

**Important:** Uses the `refresh_token` (not access_token) as the Bearer token.

**Auto-saves:** New `access_token` and `refresh_token` (token rotation).
Invalidate the current session. Returns 204 No Content.

Change the authenticated user's password.

**Side effect:** All active sessions are invalidated (user must re-login).

**Password rules:** Min 8 chars, must contain: uppercase, lowercase, digit, special character.

Request a password reset OTP. Always returns 204 (even if email doesn't exist, for security).

**In dev mode:** Check the server console for the OTP log.

Verify the OTP received via email and set a new password.

**OTP rules:** Exactly 6 digits, max 5 attempts, expires in 10 minutes.
Initiates the registration flow for a new Platform Admin by securely masking their initial creation and sending an OTP to tech@brello.co.in.
Verifies the OTP and activates the Platform Admin account.
Initiates the login flow for an existing Platform Admin by validating credentials and sending an OTP to their email.

exec: console.log('✅ Tokens saved for Platform Admin.');

request.description:
Verifies the login OTP and formally completes authentication by returning tokens.

**Auto-saves:** `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `user_id` to collection variables.

Create a new enterprise.

**Auto-saves:** `enterprise_id` to collection variables.
List all enterprises in the system.
Get a single enterprise by its UUID.
Partially update an enterprise. All fields are optional.
Delete an enterprise. Returns 204 No Content.

Create a new organization under an enterprise.

**Auto-saves:** `organization_id` to collection variables.
List all organizations.
Get a single organization by its UUID.
Get all organizations that belong to a specific enterprise.
Partially update an organization. All fields are optional.
Delete an organization. Returns 204 No Content.
Partially update an organization profile.

item.name: User & Employee
item.description: User authentication records and full Employee Aggregate Management

request.description:
Create a new user. Password is hashed server-side.

**Auto-saves:** `user_id` to collection variables.

**Password rules:**
- Minimum 8 characters
- At least one uppercase letter
- At least one lowercase letter
- At least one digit
- At least one special character (@$!%*?&)

**Phone format:** E.164 (e.g., +919876543210)
List all users. Password hash is never exposed.
Get a single user by UUID.
Partially update a user. All fields are optional. Password cannot be changed here (use auth endpoints).
Soft-delete a user (sets status to DELETED). Returns 204 No Content.

item.name: App
item.description: Application management — register apps in the multi-app architecture

request.description:
Create a new application.

**Auto-saves:** `app_id` to collection variables.

`priority` determines the default app on login (lower = higher priority).
List all registered applications, sorted by priority.
Get a single app by UUID.
Partially update an app.
Delete an app. Returns 204 No Content.

item.name: RBAC
item.description: Role-Based Access Control — roles, user-role assignments, and menu

request.description:
Create a new role scoped to an app.

**Auto-saves:** `role_id` to collection variables.
List all roles with their associated app.
Get a single role by UUID.
Get all roles belonging to a specific app.
Partially update a role.
Delete a role. Returns 204 No Content.

Assign a role to a user in a specific organization.

**This is required before the user can login!**
List all user-role assignments.
Get all role assignments for a specific user.
Get a single user-role-map assignment by UUID.
Remove a role assignment. Returns 204 No Content.

Returns the authenticated user's accessible module tree for the current app.

The response is a hierarchical tree where:
- Each node has `id`, `name`, `wbs_code`, `actions[]`, and `children[]`
- Only modules the user has permission to access are included
- Actions are filtered by both role permissions AND plan restrictions
- Tree is sorted by WBS code at each level

item.name: Document Storage
item.description: S3 Pre-signed URL operations for file uploads

request.description: Retrieve document metadata by its UUID.
Generate a short-lived S3 signed URL for downloading a protected file. Only works for ACTIVE documents.
Delete a document record. Returns 200 OK.
Get a single industry type by UUID.
Partially update an industry type.
Soft delete an industry type. Returns 204 No Content.
